title: Knema heterophylla
taxonomy: Knema heterophylla is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as nutmeg or magnolia family. The Myristicaceae family consists of about 180 species, which are mostly evergreen trees and shrubs. These plants are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, including Southeast Asia and Australia. They are known for their aromatic seeds and timber.

Its leaves and bark are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. intro: Knema heterophylla, also known as the "Borneo nut tree," is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief from symptoms and aid in the recovery process. The bioactive constituents of Knema heterophylla include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties and are responsible for its potential health benefits. In traditional medicine, Knema heterophylla is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat a range of health issues.
